registry.description: Rewrite bare IEX command tokens into a call-operator invocation over a constructed string.
forbidden_if: IEX occurs only inside strings or comments
registry.notes: Conservative token rewrite; variant still needs parse validation before acceptance.
preconditions: script contains a standalone IEX command token
semantic_invariants: scriptblock or string operand to IEX stays unchanged

registry.description: Rewrite Invoke-Expression command tokens into a call-operator invocation over a constructed string.
registry.notes: Suitable for direct command-token occurrences rather than quoted literals.
preconditions: script contains a standalone Invoke-Expression command token

registry.description: Rewrite Invoke-WebRequest command tokens into a call-operator invocation over a constructed string.
forbidden_if: script relies on shell-specific alias behavior instead of command invocation
registry.notes: Conservative command-name hiding only; does not alter URLs or flags.
preconditions: script contains a standalone Invoke-WebRequest command token

registry.description: Rewrite Start-Process command tokens into a call-operator invocation over a constructed string.
registry.notes: Useful for child-process staging patterns already present in the corpus.
preconditions: script contains a standalone Start-Process command token

registry.description: Rewrite New-Object WebClient type arguments into a constructed string expression.
forbidden_if: script already uses a nonliteral type expression
registry.notes: Conservative because it only rewrites the type-name argument to New-Object.
preconditions: script contains New-Object System.Net.WebClient or New-Object Net.WebClient
semantic_invariants: constructed .NET type name resolves to the same WebClient type

registry.description: Split quoted Invoke-Expression string literals into concatenated literals.
forbidden_if: indicator is not represented as a quoted literal
registry.notes: Intended for scripts that construct command names as data before execution.
preconditions: script contains a quoted Invoke-Expression string literal

registry.description: Split quoted Invoke-WebRequest string literals into concatenated literals.
forbidden_if: indicator is not represented as a quoted literal
registry.notes: Does not rewrite direct command-token usage.
preconditions: script contains a quoted Invoke-WebRequest string literal

registry.description: Split quoted DownloadString literals into concatenated literals.
forbidden_if: indicator is not represented as a quoted literal
registry.notes: Useful for scripts that dispatch methods or APIs through string variables.
preconditions: script contains a quoted DownloadString string literal

registry.description: Split quoted DownloadFile literals into concatenated literals.
forbidden_if: indicator is not represented as a quoted literal
registry.notes: Targets data-string representations rather than direct member-call syntax.
preconditions: script contains a quoted DownloadFile string literal

registry.description: Split quoted -EncodedCommand literals into concatenated literals.
forbidden_if: flag is not represented as a quoted literal
registry.notes: Conservative because it preserves the exact final parameter token.
preconditions: script contains a quoted -EncodedCommand string literal

registry.description: Rewrite direct DownloadString method calls into PSObject method lookup plus Invoke over a split method-name string.
forbidden_if: script already uses PSObject method dispatch
registry.notes: Aims to preserve behavior while obscuring the direct member-name literal.
preconditions: script contains a direct .DownloadString(...) method call

registry.description: Rewrite direct DownloadFile method calls into PSObject method lookup plus Invoke over a split method-name string.
forbidden_if: script already uses PSObject method dispatch
registry.notes: Preserves argument order while replacing the direct method-name literal.
preconditions: script contains a direct .DownloadFile(...) method call
semantic_invariants: download URL and output path arguments remain identical
